notes.purpose: Kaggle free-tier prototype iteration for Llama-3.1-8B + QLoRA on T4x2. Not a gate-number or release artifact environment.
notes.hardware: T4x2: two discrete T4 GPUs, 16GB each. device_map='auto' makes the 8B model span both (~32GB total addressable). This config is tuned to exploit that full 32GB budget.
notes.device_placement: device_map='auto' is single-process model sharding across both GPUs — NOT data-parallel DDP. Only one process runs; model layers are spread to fit in the combined ~32GB. QLoRA+bitsandbytes 4-bit cannot use DDP (bnb.nn.Linear4bit breaks DDP gradient gathering). Do NOT use accelerate launch or torchrun with this config.
notes.dtype: T4 is Turing-class (sm_75): fp16=true, bf16=false. bfloat16 is not supported on T4. If running on A100/L40S, use the A100/general Stage 1 config instead.
notes.memory: max_seq_len=2048 exploits the full ~32GB addressable across both T4s. gradient_accumulation_steps=16 with seq_len=2048 keeps the same ~32K tokens per gradient update as the prior (seq_len=1024, steps=32) conservative config, but with better context coverage and fewer accumulation micro-steps per update.
notes.checkpointing: save_steps/eval_steps=100 so interrupted Kaggle sessions lose less work. Sync runs/ to persistent storage or a private HF repo before the notebook ends.
notes.path_note: Paths are config-relative for train/eval data. After cloning on Kaggle, copy or mount the off-git data/ tree under /kaggle/working/ideal-spoon/data before preflight.
notes.resume: If interrupted from repo root: PYTHONPATH=code python -m llm_hawaii.train --config code/configs/stage1_fineweb2_haw_kaggle_t4x2.json --resume-from-checkpoint runs/llama31-8b-stage1-fw2-kaggle-t4x2/checkpoint-NNN
notes.oom_fallback: If Kaggle OOMs: (1) reduce max_seq_len back to 1024 and gradient_accumulation_steps back to 32 (original conservative baseline); (2) if still OOM, reduce lora_rank to 16/lora_alpha to 32; (3) for a quick plumbing test only, try max_seq_len=512. Record the config diff in the run report.
